title: pyrrocoma carthamoides var. subsquarrosa
intro: Pyrrocoma carthamoides var. subsquarrosa, also known as a rare variant of the Pyrrocoma carthamoides plant, is a type of flowering plant native to the western United States, specifically found in the mountainous regions of Colorado and Wyoming. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Pyrrocoma carthamoides var. subsquarrosa include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms associated with arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and processing the leaves and stems into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
